news.title: Bombay HC quashes order for NOC to filmmaker to merge four flats in Oshiwara society
news.para: The Bombay High Court has quashed a deputy registrar's order compelling a housing society to allow a member to merge four flats. Justice Amit Borkar ruled that the registrar exceeded his authority under the Maharashtra Co-operative Societies Act, as the power to compel NOCs for amalgamation is not vested in him.

news.title: Telangana HC suspends order setting aside e-auction of Budwel land
news.para: Telangana High Court has allowed HMDA to proceed with the e-auction of prime Budwel land, suspending a single judge's order. While the auction can proceed, the highest bidder won't be declared until the district collector decides on private parties' ownership claims. The court mandated a decision by December 12, with final auction steps after December 31.

news.title: Indian Hotels, ITC, EIH, others in race for Pune's Crowne Plaza
news.para: Indian Hotels, ITC Hotels, and EIH are among many companies interested in buying Crowne Plaza in Pune. The hotel is being sold through a bankruptcy court process. Over 40 companies have submitted expressions of interest. This sale is happening alongside another hotel, JW Marriott in Bengaluru, also under insolvency

news.title: OpenAI leases 50-seater office space with CorporatEdge in Delhi
news.para: US-based OpenAI has set up its first India office in Delhi, leasing a 50-seater workspace with premium flex operator CorporatEdge, according to two people familiar with the matter. The company had earlier announced plans to expand into India, and sources said it was particular about having a Delhi address, choosing not to locate in Gurugram or Noida.

news.title: Bombay HC restrains developer from making additions to Tardeo twin towers
news.para: The Bombay High Court has stopped developer S D Corporation from altering its Tardeo twin towers, 'The Imperial'.  Residents sued, claiming the builder planned extra flats and buildings violating the original plan. The court ruled no changes can be made without resident consent.  The builder cannot use increased buildable space from slum scheme delays to add more flats.

news.title: Sri Lotus Developers and Realty's profit falls 8% to ₹46.36 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: In a recent financial report, Sri Lotus Developers and Realty Ltd revealed a dip in net profit by 8%, now standing at Rs 46.36 crore for the September quarter. Yet, the company experienced a positive turn with total income soaring to Rs 188.81 crore, reflecting strong operational performance.

news.title: Kolte-Patil Developers posts ₹11 crore loss in Q2 FY26
news.para: In a challenging September quarter, Kolte-Patil Developers faced a net loss of Rs 11.14 crore, alongside the news of Group CEO Atul Bohra stepping down. The company's total income plummeted by 50 percent, prompting the board to appoint a new additional director and begin the search for a new CEO.

news.title: Kamdhenu's net profit grows 18% to ₹19 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Kamdhenu has celebrated an impressive 18% boost in net profits for the September quarter, reaching Rs 18.7 crore. This surge has mainly come from cost-cutting measures that have proven effective. While revenues experienced a modest uptick, the company is optimistic about rebounding after some weather-related slowdowns.

news.title: Awfis Space Solutions net profit dips 58.70% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Awfis Space Solutions reported a 58.70% decline in net consolidated profit for Q2 FY26, with profit after tax at ₹15.97 crore. Despite this, the company saw a 30.14% growth in total income, reaching ₹392.97 crore. Awfis also expanded its portfolio, adding 8,000 seats in the quarter.

news.title: Hiranandani forays into senior living, to develop one million sq ft in Chennai
news.para: Hiranandani Communities is venturing into senior living with a new project, 'Elements', in Chennai's Hiranandani Parks, Oragadam. This 4.5-acre development, a ₹300 crore venture with GTB Group, will offer 400 residences starting at ₹60 lakh.  The larger township aims for ₹4,000 crore revenue, building on its existing success.

news.title: Surya Roshni posts net profit of ₹74.19 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Surya Roshni announced a significant profit jump of 117.18 percent for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. The company's net profit reached ₹74.19 crore, a substantial increase from the previous year. Total income also saw a healthy rise of 21.60 percent.

news.title: Haryana CM emphasises strict action on civic issues in Gurugram
news.para: Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini declared Gurugram the state's fastest-growing city, vowing zero tolerance for laxity in civic amenities. He directed officials to report on water supply, sewerage, and STPs, emphasizing timely delivery of quality basic services to all residents. The CM also ordered stringent action against the tanker mafia and builders failing to provide promised amenities.

news.title: Soon, register sale deeds at any taluka office soon: Goa CM
news.para: Goa citizens will soon be able to register sale deeds at any of the 12 sub-registrar offices across the state, irrespective of the land's location. Chief Minister Pramod Sawant announced that the entire process for marriage and land registrations will be streamlined within 10 days, resolving IT infrastructure issues with NIC and ITG.

news.title: Action taken against illegal commercial constructions in Ludhiana
news.para: Ludhiana Municipal Corporation sealed three illegal shops and a building in Zone B on Monday. Additionally, an under-construction commercial building was demolished near Mohar Singh Nagar for illegal conversion from residential to commercial use. Residents are urged to obtain building plan approvals before commencing construction.

news.title: Blackstone to acquire majority in Ritz-Carlton Bengaluru from Nitesh Land
news.para: US investment powerhouse Blackstone is gearing up to strengthen its foothold in the Indian hospitality scene with plans to acquire a hefty stake in the esteemed Ritz-Carlton Bengaluru, estimated to be valued between ₹1,200 and ₹1,400 crore. This notable acquisition reflects a growing trend of international investments in India's luxury hotel landscape.

news.title: Total Environment Resorts raises ₹175 crore through NCDs
news.para: Total Environment Resorts has successfully issued ₹175 crore in listed, rated, non-convertible debentures, with Standard Chartered as the sole arranger and green co-ordinator. The proceeds will fund a residential real estate project in Bengaluru, adhering to green bond principles and SEBI regulations.

news.title: Ludhiana civic body plans to take over colonies & sites from Glada
news.para: Ludhiana Municipal Corporation is considering taking over colonies and sites from Glada, a process Glada has attempted for over a decade. This follows a similar ongoing takeover from the Ludhiana Improvement Trust. Officials are holding meetings to finalize the proposal, which was previously rejected by the MC house due to financial concerns.

news.title: Cera Sanitaryware posts net profit of ₹504.47 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Cera Sanitaryware reported a slight 0.77% dip in net profit for Q2 FY26, with profit after tax at ₹504.47 crore. Total income also saw a 16.79% decrease to ₹56.64 crore. Sanitaryware and Faucetware contributed significantly to revenues, while project sales maintained healthy momentum.

news.title: Rajasthan HC gives govt final chance to reply on 86 illegal colonies on RHB's land
news.para: The Rajasthan High Court has given the state government a final chance to respond by December 9 in a case involving 86 illegal colonies on Rajasthan Housing Board land. The court expressed strong displeasure over the government's failure to comply with previous orders to reclaim public land, despite the Supreme Court dismissing the state's appeal.

news.title: Maharashtra CM to hand over 423 flats to police families at Naigaum BDD chawls
news.para: Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis will soon hand over keys to 864 flats at Naigaum BDD chawls in Dadar. This marks a significant milestone for police families who have resided there for generations. The completed project includes five wings of 22 storeys each. Beneficiaries will receive ownership of 500-square-foot flats.
